title: Investigate and integrate destructive_command_guard into sandbox clankers
description:
Goal
Integrate destructive_command_guard (DCG) into sandbox command execution paths ("clankers") with a safe, practical policy configuration.

Reference
https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard

Scope
- Audit all clanker execution entry points where DCG should be injected
- Evaluate integration modes (wrapper, middleware, pre-exec check)
- Propose default policy and override mechanisms
- Implement integration for selected clankers
- Document policy tuning and developer workflow impacts

Acceptance Criteria
- Target clankers pass commands through DCG checks
- Default policy is defined and version-controlled
- Behavior for blocked commands is clear (message, logs, override path)
- Integration docs include how to tune policy and test blocked/allowed commands

Definition of Done
- Destructive command protection is active in agreed clanker paths with documented, testable configuration

title: Bundle br/bv release binaries in sandbox image and refresh via ags update
description:
Goal
Bundle the beads toolchain binaries inside the sandbox image so agents can use them from inside the container exactly like on the host.

Target binaries
- br (beads_rust CLI)
- bv (beads_viewer CLI)

Release sources
- beads_rust (br): https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/releases
- beads_viewer (bv): https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/releases

Scope
- Add image build/install logic to fetch/install br and bv from official GitHub releases
- Ensure both binaries are on PATH in the sandbox image
- Wire ags update to refresh installed br/bv to the latest supported release versions
- Add deterministic version verification commands for both tools
- Document failure modes (offline, missing release asset, checksum/permission issues)

Acceptance Criteria
- Fresh sandbox container has working br and bv commands on PATH
- Running ags update refreshes br and bv to the latest supported release versions
- Version verification for br and bv is documented and testable
- Failure behavior is explicit and actionable

Definition of Done
Agents running inside the sandbox can reliably run br/bv without host mounts, and operators can refresh those binaries deterministically via ags update.
close_reason: Implemented br/bv release bundling in sandbox image; ags update now resolves latest beads_rust/beads_viewer tags, rebuilds with build args, and installs verified binaries. Added docs and verification command. Smoke-tested: br 0.1.24, bv v0.15.2 inside sandbox.

title: Set up headless browser testing in sandbox using Playwright
description:
Goal
Enable headless browser tests in sandbox using Playwright (preferred stack).

Question to resolve
Clarify whether setup differs materially in sandbox/container context vs local usage.

Scope
- Define required system dependencies for Playwright in sandbox image
- Add minimal smoke test exercising browser launch and one page interaction
- Verify execution in non-interactive/containerized environment
- Document setup differences, if any (fonts, sandbox flags, deps, CI behavior)

Acceptance Criteria
- Playwright test command runs successfully in sandbox
- Required dependencies are codified in container/build config
- A short doc explains container-specific setup and known limitations
- Recommendation given for next-step expansion (e2e/visual/etc.)

Definition of Done
- Team can reliably run at least one Playwright headless smoke test from within sandbox container

title: Document notification proxy security boundaries and operator setup
description: Document how the host notification proxy works, why the host DBus session bus is not mounted, socket path expectations, permission model, and how to troubleshoot notification delivery.

title: Add psql command in container for Postgres access
description: Add a documented and/or scripted psql command that can be run from inside the container to connect to the project Postgres instance.
close_reason: Completed: added psql client to sandbox image and documented host Postgres quick-connect command.

title: Investigate Testcontainers support for integration tests inside sandbox agent container
description:
Goal
Enable an agent running inside the sandbox container to execute integration tests that rely on Testcontainers.

Context
Current feasibility is unknown (Docker-in-Podman / container runtime access constraints).

Scope
- Investigate runtime options for Testcontainers from inside sandbox container
- Build a minimal proof-of-concept integration test
- Document required container privileges, mounts, env vars, and security trade-offs
- Recommend a supported approach (or explicit non-support decision)

Acceptance Criteria
- A written feasibility report is added to docs/ with recommended approach
- PoC test is runnable via documented command
- Any required container image / compose changes are identified
- Risks and limitations are clearly documented

Definition of Done
- Team can decide Go/No-Go for full Testcontainers adoption based on documented evidence
close_reason: Investigation concluded: pursue standalone PSP project (../podman-socket-proxy, epic psp-287) and AGS integration epic agent-sandbox-3jq

title: Add host-side notification proxy for sandboxed agents
description: Provide a narrow desktop notification channel from sandboxed containers to the host user session without mounting the host DBus session bus. Scope: host-side proxy reachable over a dedicated mounted Unix socket; strict one-way notify-only protocol; explicit security boundaries.

title: Integrate external PSP (podman-socket-proxy) into AGS
description:
Objective
Integrate the standalone PSP project into AGS with minimal code ownership in this repository.

Scope in this repo
- AGS configuration and launch wiring for PSP
- Session lifecycle hooks and connectivity plumbing
- Integration tests validating AGS <-> PSP contract
- User-facing docs for enabling PSP mode in AGS

Out of Scope
- PSP broker internals (API proxy, policy engine, cleanup internals)

Dependency
Requires PSP project deliverables tracked in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287).

title: Add AGS config and run wiring for external PSP endpoint
description:
Goal
Allow AGS runs to target PSP via explicit opt-in config/flags.

Acceptance Criteria
- AGS can inject DOCKER_HOST for sandbox sessions in PSP mode
- No direct host Podman socket mount in PSP mode
- Clear errors for missing/unreachable PSP endpoint
notes: Blocked for now while proxy work is deferred pending follow-up.

title: Implement AGS-managed PSP session lifecycle hooks
description:
Goal
Coordinate AGS session metadata and PSP lifecycle expectations.

Acceptance Criteria
- AGS passes stable session identifiers/labels expected by PSP
- Normal exit and failure paths invoke cleanup hooks/notifications correctly
- Debug keep-resources mode behavior is explicit in AGS UX

title: Add AGS integration tests for PSP contract
description:
Goal
Validate AGS<->PSP integration without owning PSP internals.

Acceptance Criteria
- Happy path test proves sandbox can run a Testcontainers-based check through PSP
- Failure path test proves policy denial surfaces cleanly to AGS user
- Tests document required local preconditions

title: Document AGS PSP mode usage and boundaries
description:
Goal
Explain how AGS users enable and operate PSP mode.

Acceptance Criteria
- Docs describe setup, flags/config, and troubleshooting
- Security boundaries and responsibility split (AGS vs PSP) are explicit
- Migration path from raw-socket experiments is included

title: Document container networking for host service access
description: Ensure sandboxed agents can discover host-service routing at runtime, not only in docs. Add explicit runtime signal (env var and startup hint) that host services are reachable via host.containers.internal and localhost is container-local.
close_reason: Implemented runtime host-service discoverability in sandbox sessions (not docs-only): ags now injects AGS_HOST_SERVICES_HOST/AGS_HOST_SERVICES_HINT env vars and prints a startup hint before agent exec. Updated README/COMMANDS/TROUBLESHOOTING and added plan_build tests for env + entrypoint hint.

title: Add ephemeral auth proxy for sandbox browser opens and loopback callbacks
description:
Objective
Provide an explicit, temporary bridge for sandboxed tools that need to open host-browser URLs and optionally receive loopback HTTP callbacks back inside the container.

Scope
- Per-run mounted auth runtime directory with Unix socket path available in the sandbox
- Prompt-based allow/deny flow for browser opens on the host
- Full HTTP callback relay for loopback callback URIs
- Unmanaged open path for URLs without a detectable callback

Out of Scope
- Permanent host daemon or permanent OS URL handler registration
- Custom URI scheme callbacks
- https://localhost callbacks that require trusted local certificates
- Best-effort support for tools that bypass xdg-open/BROWSER interception entirely

title: Specify auth proxy contract, prompt UX, and unsupported flows
description:
Goal
Write the implementation spec for the temporary auth proxy before code lands.

Acceptance Criteria
- Define the host/container message protocol for URL open, callback request relay, response relay, errors, and cancellation
- Define prompt UX for callback-aware Allow/Deny and callback-less Open/Cancel flows
- Define socket discovery and retry behavior when proxy/container startup order varies
- List unsupported flows and failure modes explicitly
close_reason: Protocol spec defined in protocol.rs with ShimMessage and HostMessage types

title: Implement host auth proxy with allow/deny prompt and loopback callback capture
description:
Goal
Run a small host-side proxy that receives URL-open requests from the sandbox, prompts the user, opens the browser on approval, and optionally captures loopback callbacks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Listen on a Unix socket inside the mounted runtime dir
- Show a minimal allow/deny prompt for callback-aware flows and open/cancel prompt otherwise
- Bind the host loopback callback listener before opening the browser
- Relay the full HTTP request/response for approved callback flows
- Clean up one-shot sessions on success, deny, timeout, or disconnect

title: Wire AGS to mount a per-run auth proxy runtime dir into the sandbox
description:
Goal
Make the auth proxy runtime path available to the sandbox without requiring a fixed startup order.

Acceptance Criteria
- AGS creates a per-run temp directory on the host and bind-mounts it into the container at a stable path
- Container env/config points shims at the expected socket path inside that directory
- Mounting the directory works whether the host proxy starts before or after the container
- Runtime dir is removed when the AGS run exits

title: Add sandbox URL-open shim and callback relay for auth proxy sessions
description:
Goal
Intercept sandbox browser-open requests and replay approved callbacks into the local listener inside the container.

Acceptance Criteria
- Provide a simple xdg-open/BROWSER-compatible path for sandbox tools to request host browser opens
- Retry socket discovery briefly, then fail closed with a clear message when the host proxy is unavailable
- For approved callback sessions, replay the full HTTP request to the intended localhost target inside the container and return the response to the host proxy
- Handle multiple sessions with explicit session/request IDs

title: Add AGS integration tests for auth proxy open and callback flows
description:
Goal
Cover the temporary auth proxy behavior with automated tests.

Acceptance Criteria
- Test callback-less URL open approval/cancel behavior
- Test end-to-end loopback callback replay from host proxy to container listener
- Test clear failure when the proxy socket is absent
- Test timeout/cleanup behavior for abandoned sessions

title: Document auth proxy startup, UX, security boundaries, and troubleshooting
description:
Goal
Document how users enable and operate the temporary auth proxy.

Acceptance Criteria
- Document how to start the host proxy and what happens when it is not running
- Document prompt semantics for managed callback vs unmanaged open flows
- Document security boundaries, explicit unsupported cases, and failure modes
- Update README/config/troubleshooting docs as needed

title: Await PSP implementation deliverables from podman-socket-proxy
description: External blocker for AGS PSP work. The PSP implementation is being developed in ../podman-socket-proxy and AGS-side integration work is blocked until the external implementation and contract are ready enough to consume here. Close this when the external repo work is complete/usable.

title: Add minimal tmux setup in sandbox container for Claude 'use teams' panes
description:
Goal
Provide tmux inside the sandbox container with a modern, simple default configuration suitable for Claude Code "use teams" pane workflows.

Requested UX
- Keep default prefix Ctrl-b (do not change)
- Enable mouse support
- Improve default ergonomics/appearance without plugins
- Keep configuration intentionally minimal

Scope
- Install tmux in container image
- Add version-controlled tmux config (e.g., .tmux.conf)
- Validate pane/session behavior in sandbox context
- Document usage and any caveats

Acceptance Criteria
- tmux is available in sandbox container
- Config includes mouse support and Ctrl-b prefix
- No external tmux plugin manager dependency
- Basic team pane workflow is documented and tested

Definition of Done
- A new container started from current setup can run tmux with the configured defaults and support team pane workflows
close_reason: Added sandbox tmux support, opt-in --tmux launches, docs/completions updates, and kitty terminfo support.

title: Implement policy-gated Testcontainers broker for sandbox (no raw podman socket mount)
description:
Objective
Enable agents inside the sandbox container to run Testcontainers-based integration tests without directly mounting the host Podman socket into the sandbox.

Chosen Direction
Adopt a broker/proxy architecture (Option 2): sandbox talks to a restricted Docker-compatible broker endpoint; broker talks to rootless host Podman API and enforces policy.

Problem to Solve
Raw socket sharing works functionally but breaks sandbox isolation because commands can create arbitrary containers/mounts as the host user.

Success Criteria
- Testcontainers workflows run from inside sandbox using broker endpoint
- Broker enforces explicit policy on container/network/volume operations
- Dangerous operations are blocked with explainable errors
- Session lifecycle and cleanup are reliable
- Security model and trade-offs are documented

Out of Scope (for this epic)
- Remote/CI backend support
- Windows/macOS runtime parity beyond documented caveats
- Full multi-tenant authorization model
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Write broker architecture decision record and threat model
description:
Goal
Produce an ADR that fixes architecture boundaries before coding.

Scope
- Document trust boundaries: sandbox container, broker process, host Podman API
- Enumerate abuse cases (arbitrary bind mounts, privileged flags, host namespace escape attempts)
- Decide broker deployment model (ephemeral per `ags` run vs long-lived daemon)
- Define minimal supported Docker API surface for v1
- Define default deny-first policy posture

Deliverables
- docs/adr/xxxx-testcontainers-broker.md
- Threat model table with mitigations and residual risk
- Explicit non-goals and known limitations

Acceptance Criteria
- ADR reviewed and linked from docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
- Includes decision rationale against raw socket mounting
- Includes implementation constraints that downstream tasks can follow without ambiguity
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Implement broker MVP: Docker-compatible API fronting rootless Podman socket
description:
Goal
Build a broker service that accepts a constrained Docker-compatible API from sandbox and forwards allowed calls to rootless Podman API on host.

Scope
- Stand up broker process with Unix socket endpoint dedicated to sandbox session
- Forward core endpoints needed by Testcontainers flows (container create/start/inspect/logs/stop/rm, image pull/inspect, network create/inspect/rm)
- Normalize Podman/Docker response differences where required for clients
- Add robust request/response error mapping

Acceptance Criteria
- Broker can run a minimal create/start/wait/logs/remove container flow end-to-end
- Endpoint compatibility validated with a smoke client using Docker API calls
- Broker can connect to host rootless Podman socket without requiring privileged container flags
- Unsupported endpoints fail with explicit, structured errors
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Add policy engine to broker (deny dangerous container options by default)
description:
Goal
Enforce command-level safety controls in broker before forwarding requests to Podman.

Policy v1
- Deny privileged mode, host PID/IPC/network namespaces
- Deny host bind mounts unless path matches allowlist
- Deny device mounts and sensitive capabilities additions
- Restrict image sources/tags via allowlist or policy rules
- Enforce resource limits (pids/cpu/memory) where feasible

Scope
- Parse relevant Docker API payload fields for create/run/network operations
- Implement policy evaluation and structured deny reasons
- Provide version-controlled default policy file + schema validation

Acceptance Criteria
- Blocked requests return actionable reason + policy rule identifier
- Allow/deny behavior covered by automated unit tests
- Default policy prevents raw host filesystem escalation patterns
- Policy overrides are explicit and auditable
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Integrate brokered Testcontainers mode into ags CLI/config
description:
Goal
Expose brokered Testcontainers mode in AGS as an explicit, opt-in workflow.

Scope
- Add config section for broker mode (enabled flag, policy path, socket path, optional image allowlist override)
- Add run-time wiring so sandbox sees broker endpoint as Docker host
- Ensure default AGS mode remains unchanged (no broker unless enabled)
- Add prominent warning/help text describing changed security boundary

Acceptance Criteria
- `ags` can launch sandbox with broker mode enabled via config/flag
- Sandbox environment is configured to use broker endpoint (`DOCKER_HOST`)
- Raw host Podman socket is not directly mounted into sandbox in broker mode
- Misconfiguration errors are clear and actionable
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Solve in-sandbox host/port resolution for Testcontainers networking
description:
Goal
Make tests running inside sandbox reliably reach published container ports created through broker/Podman.

Scope
- Validate host mapping behavior for Testcontainers on Podman API from inside container
- Implement required host override strategy (env/properties/network mode)
- Validate compatibility with random host ports and parallel test runs
- Document known caveats (e.g., localhost assumptions)

Acceptance Criteria
- A reproducible test proves sandbox test process can connect to published ports
- Strategy works with at least one canonical module (e.g., Postgres or Redis)
- No manual per-test host patching required for basic path
- Troubleshooting doc includes hostname/port diagnostics
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Implement broker session lifecycle, labeling, and deterministic cleanup
description:
Goal
Ensure resources created via broker are attributable to AGS sessions and cleaned up reliably.

Scope
- Tag created containers/networks/volumes with AGS session metadata
- Implement cleanup on normal exit and crash-recovery sweep on startup
- Add TTL-based garbage collection for orphaned resources
- Support optional keep-on-failure mode for debugging

Acceptance Criteria
- No orphan resource growth in repeated test runs under normal conditions
- Crash simulation leaves resources that are recovered by next cleanup sweep
- Keep-on-failure mode is explicit and documented
- Cleanup behavior is covered by integration tests
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Add broker audit logs and operator-visible deny diagnostics
description:
Goal
Make broker decisions observable for debugging and security auditing.

Scope
- Emit structured logs for allow/deny decisions with rule IDs
- Record principal/session context and target operation metadata
- Surface concise deny messages to sandbox user while keeping detailed host logs
- Provide log redaction for sensitive payload fields

Acceptance Criteria
- Denied operations can be traced to specific policy rules
- Logs are machine-parseable and documented
- Sensitive tokens/secrets are not logged in plaintext
- Operator can diagnose common failures from docs + logs alone
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Create end-to-end brokered Testcontainers integration test suite
description:
Goal
Prove the full pipeline works: sandbox test process -> broker -> rootless Podman -> test container lifecycle.

Scope
- Add deterministic e2e tests covering happy path and blocked path
- Include at least one real Testcontainers-based integration test in Rust
- Validate parallel run behavior and cleanup guarantees
- Add CI-friendly local test target (even if CI execution deferred)

Acceptance Criteria
- Happy path test starts service container and runs assertion successfully
- Blocked path test verifies policy denial for prohibited mount/flag
- Tests assert no leaked containers/networks after run
- Test commands are documented for contributors
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Document brokered Testcontainers usage, security model, and migration path
description:
Goal
Ship user-facing and contributor-facing docs for the new brokered mode.

Scope
- Update README/docs/COMMANDS/docs/CONFIG/docs/TROUBLESHOOTING
- Add security section comparing raw socket mount vs broker mode
- Document setup, enablement flags, policy customization, and common failures
- Provide migration steps from existing raw-socket experiments

Acceptance Criteria
- New mode can be enabled by following docs alone
- Security implications are explicit and non-ambiguous
- Troubleshooting covers hostname/port mapping, denied commands, cleanup issues
- All new config fields are documented with examples
close_reason: Moved to standalone PSP project in ../podman-socket-proxy (epic psp-287)

title: Implement host user-session notification proxy
description: Implement a small host-side process that listens on a dedicated Unix socket in the user session and emits desktop notifications locally. Must not expose generic DBus access. Include schema validation, bounds checks, and reasonable failure logging.

title: Wire AGS to mount dedicated notification socket into sandbox
description: Add AGS support for the host notification proxy socket: create or discover the host socket, mount only that socket into the container at a fixed path, and expose the container path to agents/extensions. Do not mount the host session bus.

title: Write spec for host notification proxy contract and security model
description: Nail down the design before implementation. Cover: host socket path and lifecycle, container mount path, payload schema (title/body/urgency/tag), optional per-session token, max payload sizes, rate limiting, allowed operations (notify only), host backend choice (libnotify vs org.freedesktop.Notifications), error semantics, and threat model. Acceptance: a written spec with enough detail to implement and test.
